name: Hover

verdict.issue: The rule states two effects: Move changes to 20" AND the model loses the AIRCRAFT keyword. The DSL models only the stat-modifier (M → 20) and completely omits the keyword-removal of AIRCRAFT. Losing AIRCRAFT is a load-bearing game mechanic (it exits Airborne restrictions, becomes targetable normally, etc.) and is explicitly stated in the rule — this is a missing stated effect, not a secondary detail.

reasoning: Declaration timing captured via timing-is:declare-battle-formations; M set to 20 is the expressible half. Losing the AIRCRAFT keyword is an in-battle effect but keyword removal has no leaf type in the grammar — no keyword-revoke exists. The stat-modifier is encoded completely; the keyword loss is a known grammar gap. Confidence medium because the timing value is an extension and the keyword-loss arm is absent.

name: Strafing Run

verdict.issue: Correctly models the ranged +1 hit roll conditioned on the target lacking the FLY keyword. Scope self/phase is appropriate for a per-attack aura-less modifier.

reasoning: Draft used a placeholder keyword 'A' for the target filter; fix replaces it with a compound not wrapping target-has-keyword:FLY, which correctly encodes 'cannot FLY'. The ranged restriction is moved into attack_type on the modifier rather than a phase-is condition, which is more precise (covers ranged attacks in any phase). phase-is:shooting is dropped as redundant once attack_type:ranged is present.

name: Command Uplink

verdict.issue: Correctly models the trigger (stratagem-targeting), the D6 dice gate with threshold 5 ≥ (matching 'on a 5+'), and the 1 CP gain. Duration 'turn' is a reasonable envelope for a per-stratagem-use trigger.

reasoning: Draft had only the cp-gain leaf with no trigger or roll gate. The stratagem-targeting event has no dedicated condition type, so timing-is:stratagem-targeting is the closest available primitive; the reactive behavior flag signals consumers that this fires on an external event. The D6 ≥5 requirement is captured exactly with dice-gated:gte:5. cp-gain amount:1 is the on_success branch. Confidence medium because stratagem-targeting is an inferred timing value not enumerated in the spec.

name: Chaff Launcher

verdict.issue: Correctly grants the SMOKE keyword permanently to the bearer. No conditions or values in the rule to misrepresent.

reasoning: Draft used target:self which can be misread as the weapon/item granting a weapon-level keyword. The rule says 'The bearer has the SMOKE keyword' — this is a unit-level grant to the model. Fixing target to bearer and omitting weapon_type/weapon_name makes the unit-level intent unambiguous. No conditional wrapping; the keyword is permanently on the bearer. Keyword capitalised as SMOKE to match game text.

name: Scuttling Walker

verdict.issue: Correctly models all three timing conditions (normal-move, advance, fall-back) joined by OR, and the two movement-modifier effects — pass-through of friendly MONSTER/VEHICLE models and pass-through of terrain ≤ 4". Both stated movement permissions are present and scoped to self.

reasoning: Draft collapsed two distinct passthrough permissions into one vague move_type. The rule grants two separate abilities: (1) pass through friendly MONSTER/VEHICLE models, and (2) pass through terrain sections ≤4" tall. These are split into a sequence of two movement-modifier leaves. through-terrain with value:4 encodes the height ceiling; consumers interpret value as the maximum height in inches. The move timing condition is preserved from the draft. Confidence medium because the height-capped terrain modifier is an inferred convention — value on movement-modifier is not formally specified as a height filter.

name: Emanatus Force Field (Aura)

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — 4+ invulnerable save against ranged attacks, for friendly ADEPTUS MECHANICUS BATTLELINE units, within 6" (scope.range 'aura-6' credited). The duration 'battle-round' is imprecise: the rule's 'While … wholly within' phrasing denotes a continuous persistent condition across the entire game, not one that expires at the end of a battle round. 'Battle' or a persistent-while marker would be more accurate, though the practical in-game difference is narrow for a static aura.

reasoning: Draft applied the invulnerable save to target:self (the source model) under a phase-is:shooting gate, missing all three key facts. Fix: (1) scope.range=aura-6 encodes the 6" proximity — proximity is a scope, not a condition; (2) target changes to friendly-within-aura so the buff lands on the beneficiaries; (3) a compound and condition filters to ADEPTUS MECHANICUS BATTLELINE units only; (4) attack_type:ranged on the modifier restricts the invulnerable save to ranged attacks, replacing the blunt phase-is gate. threshold:4 encodes the 4+ save value, following the feel-no-pain pattern. duration:battle-round reflects that the proximity check can change with movement each round.

name: Broad spectrum data-tether

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is right (SMOKE loss + D6≥5 → 1CP). The semantic issue: `ability-grant` with `grant_type: 'loses-smoke-keyword'` inverts the grant/remove direction — losing a keyword should be a keyword-remove modifier, not an ability-grant. The effect description string salvages intent, so it reads as minor.

reasoning: SMOKE keyword removal has no `keyword-remove` type in the grammar so it is proxied via `ability-grant{grant_type:'loses-smoke-keyword'}`; the dice-gated CP gain is fully expressible once wrapped in the missing stratagem-targeting trigger condition.

name: Sentinel Directives

verdict.issue: None. Condition (Fire Overwatch Stratagem), roll-modifier setting hit threshold to 5, and scope (unit / phase, further constrained by the condition) all match the rule.

reasoning: The missing Fire Overwatch Stratagem trigger is restored as a `timing-is` condition; `operation:set` and `value:5` correctly encodes the fixed 5+ hit threshold (the 'unmodified' qualifier has no DSL field but is implicit in the absolute `set` operation), and the non-canonical `context` key from the draft is removed.

name: Leader

verdict.issue: None. Pre-battle timing, attach-to-bodyguard-unit grant, and permanent scope correctly capture the Leader attachment rule.

reasoning: The pre-battle timing condition is added to narrow activation to the setup window; `ability-grant{grant_type:'attach-to-bodyguard-unit'}` is the closest available leaf since there is no dedicated `attach` effect type, and the Attached-unit formation's in-battle consequences are expressed through downstream `is-attached` conditions on other abilities rather than here.

name: Battle Protocols
reasoning: All three protocols are encoded: Protector (+2A ranged), Conqueror (+2A melee), and Aegis (+1T), the last of which also seeds the battle-start default; the protocol-persistence mechanic ('remains until enters a different one') cannot be represented as the DSL has no state-variable concept, so `duration:battle` on scope approximates it.

name: Elevated Strider

verdict.issue: The Desperate Escape test is modeled as `leadership-modifier`. Desperate Escape is a D6-per-model wound test when falling back through enemies — it is not a Leadership or Battle-shock test. Using `leadership-modifier` misclassifies the test type, which mirrors the prohibited pattern (Leadership/Battle-shock re-roll node for a non-Leadership mechanic). The `fallback-and-act` step also appears to omit the 'Advanced' case stated in the rule, though the `leadership-modifier` error alone is sufficient to mark this wrong.

reasoning: The previously omitted Desperate Escape re-roll is added as a `leadership-modifier{test:'desperate-escape', operation:'re-roll'}` step in sequence with the already-correct `fallback-and-act` shooting eligibility.

name: Defend the Divine Work

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (4+ invulnerable for unit, lasts the phase, usable once per battle at start of any phase). Minor issue: `once-per-battle` is expressed as a `timing-is` condition alongside `start-of-phase`, but it is a usage-limit constraint, not a timing predicate. The AND composition captures the intent but the node type is semantically imprecise.

reasoning: The non-canonical `invuln_sv` key is replaced with the canonical `threshold:4`; target is corrected from `self` to `unit`; the once-per-battle usage limit is encoded as a second `timing-is{timing:'once-per-battle'}` condition AND-compounded with the `start-of-phase` trigger, and `scope.duration:phase` captures the 'until end of phase' window.

name: Stealth

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (subtract 1 from Hit roll, ranged attacks). However, the rule's condition is 'if every model in the unit has this ability' — specifically to strip the benefit when an attached CHARACTER lacks it. The authored DSL replaces that with unit-has-keyword: STEALTH, which is a keyword proxy rather than a per-model ability check, and would not correctly handle mixed-model units where one model is missing the ability.

reasoning: unit-has-keyword{STEALTH} is the canonical DSL encoding of 'all models must carry this ability', since a unit possesses a keyword only when every constituent model grants it; attack_type:ranged narrows the hit penalty to the ranged-only scope the rule specifies.

name: Focused Hunters

verdict.issue: Two core problems: (1) The re-roll in step 2 carries subset: 'all-failures', but the rule states 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' with no restriction on outcome — the all-failures filter is a phantom condition absent from the rule. (2) Step 2 has no condition linking the re-roll to attacks that target the selected quarry unit; the rule grants the re-roll only 'each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets that unit', so without that target-condition the authored DSL gives a blanket hit re-roll against any target.

reasoning: The pre-battle selection is encoded as a battle-start trigger granting the quarry-designation ability; the re-roll is a persistent second step, but the constraint that it applies only when attacking the designated quarry cannot be fully expressed because the DSL has no runtime-designation condition type — this residue is irresolvable without a grammar extension.

name: Canticles of the Omnissiah

verdict.issue: Trigger (start of Command phase) and effect (select/grant a Canticle ability) are correctly modelled. Scope duration 'battle-round' is a slight imprecision: the rule says 'until the start of your next Command phase', which is not exactly one full battle round — it begins mid-round at the Command phase, not at the round boundary. Functionally close but not precise.

reasoning: Wrapping in phase-is:command ∧ timing-is:start precisely captures the 'at the start of your Command phase' trigger; the illegal selection_required key is dropped and only the canonical grant_type key is retained; duration battle-round reflects the 'until start of your next Command phase' window.

name: Mechanicus Bodyguard

verdict.issue: Condition (within 3" of friendly ADEPTUS MECHANICUS), effect (grant Lone Operative), target (self), and scope are all correct.

reasoning: Replacing the unconditional draft with a unit-within-range-of condition keyed to range:3 and friendly-adeptus-mechanicus expresses the 'while within 3"' proximity requirement the draft omitted; the illegal ability_id key is replaced with the canonical grant_type.

name: Network Override

verdict.issue: Outer TECH-PRIEST condition correctly gates both bullets. Bullet 1 (action eligible after advancing) is wrapped in the advanced-this-turn conditional. Bullet 2's grant type 'shoot-while-actioning' encodes the 'while performing an Action' condition semantically in its name, which is an acceptable compact representation. No phantom conditions; no missing conditions beyond what is captured in the grant type.

reasoning: The two bullet clauses become a sequence inside the TECH-PRIEST guard: clause 1 is gated by advanced-this-turn (the missing effect), clause 2 is an unconditional ability-grant for shoot-while-actioning because the DSL has no 'has-started-action-this-turn' condition type — that residue is semantically captured by the grant_type label rather than a formal condition.

name: ATTACHED UNIT

verdict.issue: Representing attachment-eligibility re-routing as a named ability-grant (kataphron-breachers-leader-eligibility) is a sound approach for a list-construction meta-rule. The conditional structure ('if a CHARACTER with Leader can attach to KATAPHRON BREACHERS, it can attach here instead') is implicit in the grant type's semantics. No phantom conditions; no missing core mechanic.

reasoning: This is an army-construction leader-pairing rule — it governs which CHARACTER units may attach to this unit at list-building time rather than producing any in-battle effect — so it falls under the unencodable army-construction category; the best partial encoding captures the eligibility grant semantically via grant_type.

name: Blistering Salvoes

verdict.issue: Both branches correctly gate the hit +1 on the matching keyword (INFANTRY for belleros, MONSTER/VEHICLE for ferrumite) and scope the modifier to the right weapon via weapon_name. OR operator on the ferrumite branch is correct.

reasoning: Two independent attack-scoped bonuses each locked to their weapon_name and target-keyword guard; a sequence carries both simultaneously so neither crowds out the other.

name: Dread Snipers

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (force a Battle-shock test on a hit enemy unit after shooting) is correct. Two secondary imprecisions: (1) effect type 'leadership-modifier' with only {test:'battle-shock'} and no operation/value is the DSL's 'force a test' encoding, which is structurally distinct from the modifier pattern used in neurostatic-interference-aura — the type is a slightly loose fit for 'must take a test'; (2) the rule grants 'select one enemy unit' (player choice among all hit targets), whereas modeling it as a per-attack condition on 'defender' could imply the test applies to every individually-hit unit rather than one chosen unit. Neither changes the core effect.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack {subject:'target'} encodes the post-shot 'hit one or more times' prerequisite; defender is the specific enemy unit selected after the shot resolves.

name: Achillan Eye

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: re-roll wound roll, right weapons, right keyword conditions. 'subset: all-failures' is technically more restrictive than the rule's 'you can re-roll the Wound roll', which permits re-rolling any result (including a success) — the rule imposes no failure-only restriction. In practice this never matters, but the DSL adds a constraint the rule does not state.

reasoning: Parallel structure to blistering-salvoes: each sequence arm locked to its weapon_name and a distinct target-keyword guard, so both weapons operate independently with no condition bleed.

name: Infiltrators

verdict.issue: 'ability-grant' with grant_type 'infiltrate' is the standard DSL abstraction for the 9"-from-enemy-deployment-zone-and-all-enemy-models deployment rule. Scope range=unit (all models must have the ability) and duration=permanent are correct.

reasoning: The deployment capability is encodable as ability-grant {grant_type:'infiltrate'}. The 'every model in the unit must have this ability' precondition has no matching condition type in the grammar and cannot be expressed; ability-grant is the correct leaf over movement-modifier (which requires a value field the infiltrate rule does not have).

name: Voices in the Code

verdict.issue: Timing (start of fight phase), trigger (engagement-state), target (enemy-within-aura filtered by engagement condition), and effect (battle-shock test) all match the rule. Uses the same 'leadership-modifier' force-test pattern as dread-snipers — consistent within the DSL and correctly applied.

reasoning: engagement-state condition directly encodes the Engagement Range requirement; timing-is:start combined with phase-is:fight pins the trigger to the exact window, preventing the vague 'during fight phase' reading of the draft.

name: Neurostatic Interference (Aura)

verdict.issue: Exclusive two-branch logic correctly models the 'instead' clause: base -1 fires only when NOT near friendly AM BATTLELINE, enhanced -2 fires when near BATTLELINE — preventing additive stacking. Values, operations, target, and aura-6 scope all match the rule. No phantom conditions.

reasoning: Two explicit mutually exclusive arms replace the opaque nesting: base -1 fires when no BATTLELINE support is present (NOT guard), enhanced -2 fires when it is; the not-compound prevents double-application and makes both penalties legible at a glance.

name: Deep Strike

verdict.issue: Faithfully models both rule sentences: step 1 (ability-grant setup-in-reserves) maps to 'can be set up in Reserves instead of on the battlefield,' step 2 (deep-strike effect) maps to 'Reinforcements step, more than 9" from all enemy models.' Scope unit/battle is correct for a permanent unit ability.

reasoning: The deep-strike leaf canonically covers both reserves-entry and the 9" Reinforcements-step placement, but the verifier gap is resolved by making reserves-entry explicit as an ability-grant prefix in a sequence, leaving deep-strike to carry the deployment-step semantics.

name: Aerial Deployment

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — HOVER keyword + in-strategic-reserves condition gates a rounds-1-to-3 deployment grant, scope self/battle is right. The 'regardless of any mission rules' override is not represented in the DSL, which is a meaningful qualifier (it overrides mission restrictions that would otherwise block early Strategic Reserves arrival).

reasoning: HOVER mode and Strategic Reserves are encoded as a compound precondition; the three-round deployment window (rounds 1–3, ignoring mission rules) is a named rule deviation with no battle-round-number condition in the DSL, so ability-grant with a descriptive kebab captures it without inventing unsupported fields.

name: Rad-saturation (Aura)
reasoning: OC is a canonical DSL stat; the VEHICLE exclusion is a not-compound condition on the affected unit; the 3" radius lives in scope.range as aura-3, and enemy-within-aura correctly identifies the recipients.

name: Enhanced data-tether

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — targeted-by-stratagem trigger, D6 ≥5 for +1CP. Scope duration is 'turn' but the rule is an always-on passive that fires each time across the whole game; duration should be 'battle.'

reasoning: The stratagem-targeting event is the reactive trigger, encoded as timing-is{targeted-by-stratagem} (closest available condition); the 5+ roll is a dice-gated gate, and cp-gain fires only on success.

name: Lord of the Machine Cult

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — is-attached conditions correctly gate FNP 5+ (non-ELECTRO-PRIESTS) and FNP 4+ (ELECTRO-PRIESTS), mutually exclusive. Scope duration is 'turn' but the rule says 'while this model is leading a unit,' an ongoing aura that lasts the whole battle; duration should be 'battle' (or 'while-attached').

reasoning: The DSL has no else-branch, so the two mutually exclusive FNP thresholds are modeled as a sequence of two conditionals: is-attached AND NOT ELECTRO-PRIESTS fires FNP 5+, is-attached AND ELECTRO-PRIESTS fires FNP 4+, exactly one can be true at any time.

name: Dataspike

verdict.issue: All conditions present (fight phase, start-of-phase, VEHICLE keyword, engagement-state). D6 ≥4 gates D6 mortal wounds to defender and roll-modifier hit -1 melee on defender — correctly represents 'WS worsened by 1' as -1 to hit. Scope phase matches the rule's 'until end of the phase' duration. No phantom conditions, no missing conditions.

reasoning: VEHICLE target requirement and engagement-state are added to the condition; the 4+ gate drives a sequence of D6 mortal wounds plus a melee hit-roll −1 on the defender — WS is not a DSL unit stat, so the Weapon Skill debuff is encoded as its observable equivalent: subtract 1 from the enemy's melee hit rolls for the phase.

name: Ride the Thermals

verdict.issue: The 12" option is gated on a pre-move condition (unit is currently within 6" of friendly BATTLELINE). The rule's constraint is an end-of-move restriction: every model must *end* that move wholly within 6" of BATTLELINE — a player can start anywhere and move toward BATTLELINE to qualify. Modeling it as a precondition blocks the 12" move whenever the unit isn't already adjacent to BATTLELINE, which is mechanically opposite to the rule's intent.

reasoning: Outer AND gates on shooting phase, after-unit-shot timing, and NOT(in-engagement-range). Option 1 is an unconditional 6" normal move plus no-charge restriction. Option 2 nests a conditional that gates the 12" move on unit-within-range-of friendly BATTLELINE (range:6 added, target_type corrected from 'friendly-keyword' to 'friendly' with keyword sub-param); the no-charge restriction sits inside that conditional so it only applies when option 2 is actually available and taken. Both options apply no-charge because 'if it does' applies equally.

name: Breaching Command

verdict.issue: The enhanced branch (near BATTLELINE) uses subset: "all-failures", but the rule says "you can re-roll the Hit roll" with no qualifier — meaning any result, including a successful hit. "all-failures" restricts re-rolls to failed hits only. The core structure (baseline re-roll-1s / enhanced full re-roll near BATTLELINE) is correct; the subset value is slightly too narrow.

reasoning: Two mutually exclusive conditionals in sequence: when NOT within 6" of friendly ADEPTUS MECHANICUS BATTLELINE, re-roll hit roll of 1 (subset:ones); when within 6", re-roll the full hit roll instead (subset:all-failures). Exactly one branch fires at any given moment. The draft's flat re-roll entirely omitted the BATTLELINE upgrade branch.

name: Optimised Gait

verdict.issue: Advance is modeled as a movement-modifier in both branches, but "Advance rolls" are dice rolls (the D6 added to the Move characteristic), not movement-distance modifiers. Charge is correctly modeled as roll-modifier; Advance should match. Values (+1/+2) and conditions are correct.

reasoning: Two mutually exclusive conditionals encode '+1 vs +2' based on BATTLELINE proximity. Charge roll bonus uses roll-modifier{roll:charge}. Advance roll bonus uses movement-modifier{move_type:advance} — adding to the Advance D6 roll is net-equivalent to added advance distance. The draft used friendly-within-aura as target (wrong — the condition is on self's proximity, not an aura effect), emitted only the charge modifier, and omitted both the advance modifier and the +2 BATTLELINE-upgrade branch.

name: Electro-shock

verdict.issue: The Advance debuff uses movement-modifier (value: -2) instead of a roll-modifier, mirroring the type error in optimised-gait. The rule says "subtract 2 from Advance and Charge rolls" — both are roll modifiers. Charge is correctly typed; Advance is not. All values, conditions, and scope duration ("battle-round" is equivalent to "until the end of your opponent's next turn" when triggered during your own turn) are correct.

reasoning: Condition AND: after-unit-shot in shooting phase, the target was hit by bearer's attacks (subject:target, count_min:1), excluding MONSTER and VEHICLE. Effect sequence on defender fully encodes 'shocked': subtract 2 from M characteristic, subtract 2 from charge roll, subtract 2 from advance distance. Duration battle-round is the closest encoding for 'until end of opponent's next turn'. The draft's vague ability-grant is replaced with the complete mechanical decomposition of the shocked state.

name: Tactica Obliqua

verdict.issue: Two separate errors: (1) The D6 option is modeled as dice-gated (roll ≥ 1, which is always true) with a fixed value of 6" — the rule gives a variable move of *up to D6"*, meaning the roll caps the distance, not unlocks a fixed 6" move. (2) The 6" guarded option uses a pre-move condition (unit currently within 6" of BATTLELINE) rather than an end-of-move constraint, the same category error as ride-the-thermals.

reasoning: Outer AND: once-per-turn, enemy unit ends a Normal/Advance/Fall-Back move within 9" (opponent-unit-within-range with trigger and move_types params), unit NOT in engagement range. Choice option 1 uses dice-gated(D6 ≥1, always fires) with value:6 as a bounded approximation of 'up to D6"' — DSL value must be an int and D6 is bounded by 6; confidence is medium because the die result as movement cap is not natively representable. Option 2 is a conditional 6" normal move gated on proximity to friendly BATTLELINE. The draft's single movement-modifier with an invalid trigger field is replaced with the full reactive-conditional + two-option choice structure.

name: Dynamic Efficiency

verdict.issue: Both parts are correctly modeled: charge eligibility after Advance/Fall Back as an ability-grant, and the Desperate Escape re-roll as a leadership-modifier (not a combat re-roll). Scope duration "permanent" is appropriate for a passive always-on ability.

reasoning: Sequence of two permanent passive effects. ability-grant{grant_type:charge-after-advance-or-fallback} encodes charge eligibility after either advancing or falling back in a single grant. leadership-modifier{test:desperate-escape, operation:re-roll} encodes Desperate Escape test re-rolls following the identical pattern the spec prescribes for battle-shock re-rolls. The draft's single ability-grant omitted the Desperate Escape re-roll entirely.

name: Line-breakers
reasoning: Per-model iteration cannot be directly expressed, so each dice-gated node represents the roll made once per model in Engagement Range; the two mutually-exclusive conditional branches encode the +2 proximity bonus by shifting the effective threshold from 4 to 2.

name: Enginseer
reasoning: Corrects both flaws: the 3" VEHICLE proximity is a triggering condition (not a scope aura), and the not-is-attached guard captures the 'unless leading a unit' clause.

name: Omnissiah’s Blessing
reasoning: Wound restoration has no dedicated leaf so dice-gated D3 on threshold 1 (always fires) signals the variable healing quantum; the VEHICLE-conditional feel-no-pain step captures the persistent 5+ that is distinct from the wound heal.

name: Vengeance for the Omnissiah
reasoning: Simplifies the compound condition to units-destroyed with friendly VEHICLE keyword narrowing, moves the 12" proximity into scope.range per DSL convention, and uses weapon_name to make the Attacks set apply only to the Omnissian axe.

name: Servo-skull Uplink
reasoning: Encodes the once-per-battle and phase-start timing as compound conditions, gates target selection on SKITARII keyword and battle-shocked state, and uses ability-grant to express the state-removal effect that has no dedicated clear-shock leaf in the DSL.

name: Firing Deck 2
reasoning: Wraps the grant in a shooting-phase condition to fix the missing trigger scope; the grant_type name encodes the capacity-2 variant of firing-deck; full embarkation targeting logic is not directly expressible and is implied by the named mechanic.
